content.problemStatement:
There are NNN slimes arranged in a line, and the attribute of the iii-th slime can be represented by a tuple (Ai,Bi)(A_i, B_i)(Ai‚Äč,Bi‚Äč).

You need to perform the following operations exactly N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 times. At the KKK-th (1‚Č§K‚Č§N‚ąí1)(1 \le K \le N-1)(1‚Č§K‚Č§N‚ąí1) operation:


Choose two adjacent indices iii, jjj (1‚Č§i,j‚Č§N‚ąíK+11 \leq i, j \leq N-K+11‚Č§i,j‚Č§N‚ąíK+1, ‚ą£i‚ąíj‚ą£=1|i - j| = 1‚ą£i‚ąíj‚ą£=1), and let the iii-th slime eat the jjj-th slime.
After this operation, the attribute of the iii-th slime becomes (Ai+Bj,Bi‚ąíAj‚ąíBj)(A_i + B_j, B_i - A_j - B_j)(Ai‚Äč+Bj‚Äč,Bi‚Äč‚ąíAj‚Äč‚ąíBj‚Äč) (and the jjj-th slime disappears). Note that after each operation, the number of slimes decreases by 111, and all slimes are reindexed starting from 111. The relative order between uneaten slimes remains unchanged.
After these operations, only one slime (A1,B1)(A_1, B_1)(A1‚Äč,B1‚Äč) remains. Find the maximum value of (A1+B1)(A_1 + B_1)(A1‚Äč+B1‚Äč).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN ‚ÄĒ the number of slimes.
The next NNN lines describe the slimes. The iii-th of these NNN lines contains two space-separated integers AiA_iAi‚Äč and BiB_iBi‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output the maximum value of the final (A1+B1)(A_1 + B_1)(A1‚Äč+B1‚Äč).
constraints: The sum of N2N^2N2 over all test cases won't exceed 2.5‚čÖ1052.5 \cdot 10^52.5‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111:


If you choose slime 111 to eat slime 222, the attribute of slime 111 becomes (A1+B2,B1‚ąíA2‚ąíB2)=(1+4,2‚ąí3‚ąí4)=(5,‚ąí5)(A_1+B_2,B_1-A_2-B_2)=(1+4,2-3-4)=(5,-5)(A1‚Äč+B2‚Äč,B1‚Äč‚ąíA2‚Äč‚ąíB2‚Äč)=(1+4,2‚ąí3‚ąí4)=(5,‚ąí5).


If you choose slime 222 to eat slime 111, the attribute of slime 222 becomes (A2+B1,B2‚ąíA1‚ąíB1)=(3+2,4‚ąí1‚ąí2)=(5,1)(A_2+B_1,B_2-A_1-B_1)=(3+2,4-1-2)=(5,1)(A2‚Äč+B1‚Äč,B2‚Äč‚ąíA1‚Äč‚ąíB1‚Äč)=(3+2,4‚ąí1‚ąí2)=(5,1).


Thus, it is optimal to choose slime 222 to eat slime 111. The answer is 5+1=65+1=65+1=6.
